title: Forza Horizon 6

notes:
First it complained my install path was too long, so I moved it to the root of a secondary SSD.

Then it asked me to sign in with a Microsoft account despite playing on Steam, and I had to scan my face to verify my age.

Then, after doing shader compilation, it crashes consistently on a loading screen where it shows me the controls on an Xbox One controller.
title: Forza Horizon 6

notes:
- Crashes ~3sec into 3D intro drive (cutscene plays fine). GPU disconnects,
audio loops, render thread dies. No exception dialog. Straight up fully hangs.

- PROTON_LOG=1 shows direct NT syscalls from forzahorizon6.exe: sigsys_handler SIGSYS rax 0x50/0x93 -> EXCEPTION_ILLEGAL_INSTRUCTION at 0x145356164 -> 296k+ recursive seh:dispatch_exception.

- Game uses syscall opcodes for anti-tamper/anti-hook. Wine converts to ILLEGAL_INSTRUCTION, game's handler loops forever. Deterministic .text offset across Proton versions.

- Tested: Proton 10 stable, proton-cachyos 11, GE-Proton all crash the same. Tried: PROTON_DISABLE_NVAPI=1, NVAPI driver version override, prefix wipe + regen. None helped.

- Hardware: RTX 3090, Ryzen 7 5700X, NVIDIA 595.71.05, CachyOS kernel 7.0.5-1-cachyos, Hyprland.

- AMD Linux reports green/gold same day. NVIDIA Windows shipped 596.xx game-ready driver / NVIDIA Linux stuck at 595.71.05 in all channels (stable/beta/open/vulkan-beta). Strong hypothesis: missing 596 fixes on Linux side. Will retest when NVIDIA releases 596.xx Linux.

- FH5 had identical pattern at 2021 launch: fixed weeks later via driver + Proton updates. History basically repeating.
title: Forza Horizon 6

notes: (NVIDIA GPU) - Verschiedene Proton versionen und diverse Umgebungsvariablen getestet, alles ohne Erfolg. Spiel startet und zeigt sämtliche Videosequenzen bis dahin, wo man selbst spielen kann, dann crasht es.

title: Forza Horizon 6

notes:
In the current state, running it on my PCis not possible.
WIthout gamescope, the game crashes Hyprland when in Fullscreen. In Windowed mode it scales to 1920x1080 and isn't movable between screen, nor focuasble in Hyprland. Launch options to select monitor and window size don't influence it.

With gamescope it launches but setting the game to fullscreen causes it to blackscreen until leaving fullscreen again.

Gamescope+Windowed works but doesn't scale right. When setting the resolution back and forth between different resolutions, you can get it to scale right.

The nvidia-open and the proprietary drivers both cause this issue.

Once you've dealt with this, you'll get a FHC000 error after starting to drive in the tutorial.

gpu: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3070 Ti Laptop GPU
notes:
Game immediately pops up to tell you to install the latest drivers (596.x). Latest Linux proprietary Nvidia drivers are still 595.x. You can go through and link your Microsoft account, mess with settings, start the game, watch the opening cutscene, but after about 5 seconds of actual gameplay the display driver (Nvidia prop driver) will crash thus causing the game to crash. The issue is pretty obvious. The Nvidia prop drivers for Linux are not up to date enough to utilize some kind of weird probably unnecessary technology that exists in 596.x, but not 595.x.

Game is Deck Verified, which implies it should work fine on systems using an AMD GPU. As proven in other reports here, this seems to be the case. Just don't buy this game if you have an Nvidia card until the 596.x drivers land on Linux and it is verified to work properly under Proton.
title: Forza Horizon 6
